{"step_by_step_thinking": "Eisenmenger's complex is a severe complication of congenital heart disease characterized by high pulmonary artery pressure and right-to-left shunting of blood. It leads to chronic hypoxemia and right ventricular failure, which can result in significant maternal morbidity and mortality, especially during pregnancy. The other heart diseases mentioned, such as coarctation of the aorta, mitral stenosis, and aortic stenosis, while serious, do not have the same level of maternal mortality associated with them as Eisenmenger's complex.", "answer_choice": "A"}
